hypothesis_en: Mr President, Mr Dora Barroso, allow me first of all to congratulate you on your appointment as President of the European Commission and, of course, also to make some comments on three points which I think are important: firstly, the role of the Commission and the appointment of its President, and secondly, Firstly, the role of the Commission and the appointment of its President. With the new constitutional treaty, which will come into force in the next five years of its responsibility and also of this House, this issue will change and it will be the European Council, taking into account the outcome of the elections, that will propose to Parliament the candidate to be elected. I think it is a step forward, although I still consider it not a decisive step, and I would like to ask you, Mr Barroso, whether you think that with the new constitutional treaty the Commission is really going to become a real executive government of the Union. They will have to vote in the elections to the European Parliament, they will feel that their vote is decisive and that by voting they will be electing the president who will govern the Union in the next term. I would like to ask you whether, in a similar situation, in your new responsibility, you are going to allow members of the European Union to take such a decision without first consulting the Council, without having previously taken a position. It speaks of the powers of the European institutions, of the institutions of the Union, of the institutions of the Union, of the institutions of the Union, of the institutions of the Union, of the institutions of the Union. What do you intend to do in your mandate to really integrate into the European Union the real institutional and cultural diversity of the Union?
reference_en: thank you very much Sir (1.68) (1.13) Mr Barroso may I begin by congratulating you on your nomination as President-designate of the European Commission and I would also like to comment now on three points that seem to me to be germane first of all the role of the Commission the achievements of its President I'd also like to talk about CFSP and I'd also like to talk about sovereignty subsidiarity and the sharing of powers in the European Union first of all then the role of the Commission and the appointment of its President (1.56) now you have been designated by the Heads of State the European Council and it now falls to this House the European Parliament to approve that appointment (2.54) so that (1.34) you come forward supported by some of the Member States o= of the Union now over the next five years I think that pattern will change and the European Council will have to take account of election results and put forward to the Parliament a President-designate of the Commission to be fully elected by the European Parliament and that seems to me to constitute progress a= although it's perhaps not a a decisive step forward in itself but Mr Barroso do you believe that with the new Constitutional Treaty (1.29) the Commission will turn into an executive government of the Union do you believe that citizens when they vote for the European Parliament do you believe that they will feel that their vote is decisive and that their vote will in turn serve to vote in the President of the Commission to govern the European Union for the next term of office now it seems to me (1.51) that the reason why we had such a low turnout recently is because our citizens don't feel that they are engaged in that way (1.36) after all you hosted a meeting in the Azores that triggered off the war and invasion of Iraq and (1.27) if there were a similar situation in your (1.1) new position would you (1.11) allow Members of the EU to take a similar decision before consulting the Council before there is an established common position of all the Members of the European Union and furthermore on diversity the Constitutional Treaty talks about the powers of European institutions and State institutions it talks about exclusive powers falling to the Union and shared powers but ther= there is (1.28) ~a_n area that hasn't been covered now there are States which have constitutionally based powers that the governments the central governments of those States may not determine and how do you think the European Union can properly reflect the cultural and constitutional diversity of the whole Union thank you
source_es: muchas gracias señor Presidente Señor Durão Barroso permítame en primer lugar felicitarle por su designación para el ~c_argo de Presidente futuro Presidente de la Comisión Europea y también permítame le ruego que le haga algunas consideraciones al respecto sobre tres (1.08) puntos que me parecen importantes en primer lugar sobre el papel de la Comisión y el nombramiento de su presidente en segundo lugar sobre la política exterior y de ~segridad común y en tercer lugar sobre la diversidad subsidiariedad y las soberanías compartidas en la Unión Europea (2.21) en primer lugar el papel de la Comisión y el nombramiento de su presidente ahora mismo usted ha sido designado (1.14) por el= los Jefes de Gobierno por el Consejo Europeo y corresponde al Parlamento Europ= a este Parlamento a esta Cámara aprobar su designación que finalmente será nominada será nombrada junto con el resto de miembros de la Comisión por parte de los Gobiernos de los Estados miembros con el nuevo Tratado Constitucional que entrará en vigor en estos cinco años de su próxima responsabilidad y también de la de esta Cámara esta cuestión va a cambiar y será el Consejo Europeo el que teniendo en cuenta el resultado de las elecciones propondrá al Parlamento el candidato a ser elegido Presidente de la Comisión que será elegido por el Parlamento Europeo me parece un paso adelante aunque todavía considero que no es un paso decisivo yo quisiera preguntarle a usted Señor Barroso si piensa (1.24) que con el nuevo Tratado Constitucional realmente la Comisión se va a convertir en un verdadero Gobierno ejecutivo de la Unión si piensa usted que realmente los ciudadanos cuando tengan que depositar su voto en las elecciones al Parlamento Europeo van a sentir que su voto es decisorio y que con su voto van a estar eligiendo al ~presidiente que va a gobernar la Unión en el próximo periodo hemos tenido una experiencia que ha sido absolutamente negativa porque ha habido una gran abstención y yo creo que es porque los ~ciundaanos no han sentido que su voto servía para esto en segundo lugar usted fue anfitrión del encuentro en las Azores que determinó la invasión y la guerra en Iraq yo le quería preguntar si en el= en una situación similar en su nueva responsabilidad va a permitir que m= miembros de la Unión Europea tomen una decisión semejante sin haber primero consultado al Consejo sin haber habido pri= previamente una posición conjunta de todos los miembros de la Unión Europea -itucional habla de las competencias del Parl= de las instituciones europeas de las instituciones también estatales habla de competencias exclusivas de la Unión y de competencias compartidas pero existe una diversidad real interior en la Unión que no ha sido contemplada son las naciones sin Estado son las regiones constitucionales que tienen competencias sobre las cuales no pueden decidir los Gobiernos centrales de los Estados qué piensa usted hacer en su mandato para integrar realmente en la Unión Europea la diversidad real institucional y cultural de la Unión muchas gracias
